pr-evidence/vllm-runtime-preflight/pr-ev.gpt55_review_summary: Reviewer found two P2 issues: handshake metadata falsely defaulted provider_factory to the Cachet native factory, and --layer-names-json only accepted a file path despite help promising a JSON value. The fixes stamp the actual configured provider factory onto native providers, make custom factory paths fail the strict preflight, and support both inline and file-backed layer-name JSON; the reviewer rechecked those fixes and approved PR #314.
verification: python -m pytest -q tests/test_vllm_kv_injection_vllm_runtime_preflight.py tests/test_vllm_kv_injection_vllm_native_provider.py tests/test_vllm_kv_injection_vllm_runtime_contract.py tests/test_vllm_kv_injection_probe.py tests/test_vllm_kv_injection_vllm_dynamic_connector.py tests/test_native_probe_factories.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py (158 passed)
what_changed: Added a strict vLLM native-runtime preflight record and CLI that combines installed vLLM connector contract diagnostics, provider factory identity, and registered layer-name mapping validation; wired DocumentKVNativeProvider handshake metadata to emit the same preflight record.
pr-evidence/vllm-runtime-preflight/pr-ev.why: Before launching provider-backed native probes or g5/g6 benchmark runs, Cachet needs a package-owned target-runtime gate that fails closed when the installed vLLM API, provider factory wiring, or layer-name mapping is not release-safe.
